commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Hale, or healthy. Hebrew, "in perfect strength. "(Haydock)
Septuagint, "simplicity, or folly. "St. Augustine reads with the old Vulgate, "in the strength of his simplicity, (Calmet) or innocence. (Haydock)
These outward appearances prove nothing for interior piety or wickedness. (Calmet)

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: 62. These things being so, who may investigate the secrets of Almighty God, to find out wherefore He permits them so to be? Now to the Elect and the Lost, their life indeed is unlike, but the corruption of the flesh in death is no way unlike.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
